text: 7. SAFETY: Subcontractor is to adhere to their Company's Safety Program or OSHA requirements, whichever is more stringent. Subcontractor  is  responsible  to  meet  or  exceed  all  OSHA  Haz/Com  and  other  Safety  Program  requirements.  Subcontractor's Safety Program is to be kept on site in the Subcontractor's vehicle(s), tool boxes, containers, etc. Subcontractor is required to produce these documents at the request of the Contractor. Subcontractor shall provide SDS information for all materials brought onto the site. Subcontractor shall provide flagmen/traffic control as necessary to complete scope of work.
